still_paying_notes: Quebec special tobacco tax funded repayment; final payment November 2006 (~C$1.61B total inc. interest & repairs). Coined "The Big Owe".
Congressional bailout of Lake Placid Organizing Committee; USOC & federal funds covered shortfall.
Debt absorbed by USSR federal budget; hard to isolate post-1991 successor obligations.
"Bosnian War (1992-95) destroyed most venues; no formal debt-repayment record."
First fully privately-financed modern Games; USD 215-250M surplus, no public debt.
OCO'88 closed with C$90M surplus; endowed Calgary Olympic Development Association (still operating today).
Reported ~USD 300M+ surplus; used to seed Korea Sports Promotion Foundation.
Savoie département required regional & national bailouts through the 1990s; debt reported still on regional books through 2000s.
Public share ~61% (city 20%, region 20%, national 21%). City & national debt attributed to Games serviced into the 2010s.
Central-government funded; no dedicated Olympic debt vehicle survives.
ACOG operationally balanced; residual public infrastructure debt small vs. total.
Nagano Prefecture bonds for Shinkansen & venues serviced into mid-2010s; ~JPY 100B remaining as of 2008.
NSW Auditor-General reported ~AUD 2.4B net public cost; venue operating losses continued 10+ years.
USD ~USD 100M surplus; federal contribution ~USD 1.3B (GAO 2001).

Greek state carries the Olympic cost as part of its public-debt overhang cited in Troika/IMF programmes; ~EUR 9B added to national debt (Bank of Greece estimate). Considered contributing factor to 2010-2018 debt crisis.
Turin Organizing Committee closed with ~EUR 34M deficit; Piemonte servicing venue-maintenance debt through 2020s (per Corte dei Conti).
Chinese National Audit Office 2009 report; central & Beijing municipal funding not broken out publicly.
VANOC declared debt-free 2014; BC provincial share ~CAD 925M absorbed.
Public Sector Funding Package GBP 9.3B; National Audit Office final report 2012.
State bank VEB.RF holds ~RUB 240B of non-performing Sochi Olympic loans as of 2024 (Reuters, Vedomosti). Federal budget continues to service.
State of Rio declared financial emergency (Decree 45.692/2016) two months before Games; austerity & federal bailout through 2020s. Museum-of-Tomorrow & venue maintenance debts unresolved.
Gangwon Provincial Government reported ~KRW 96B/year venue operating losses through 2023; central-government subsidies ongoing.
Board of Audit of Japan 2022 final report: total JPY 1.42T (~USD 13B); Tokyo & national government split 60/40 of public share. Debt-service tail projected through 2030s.
State-financed; no public breakdown of Olympic debt.
French Cour des comptes June 2025: EUR 6B taxpayer contribution; OCOG closed with EUR 76M surplus.
